## Maryland Energy Administration (MEA)
**Source:** http://energy.maryland.gov/govt/Pages/smartenergycommunities.aspx
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** 

### Local Government Energy Modernization Program (L-GEM)
- **Type:** Grant
- **Financial Details:** $64 million total: $50 million anticipated for planning activities and renewable energy projects, $10 million anticipated for energy efficiency projects, $4 million anticipated for electrification projects
- **Eligibility:** Only incorporated towns, cities, and counties of Maryland may apply. Counties that apply for AOI1 are not eligible to apply for AOI2 or AOI3.
- **How to Apply:** Application Deadline: 5:00 P.M. ET, December 5, 2025; interested applicants should check the kick-off webinar and FAQ document on the MEA website
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial, Agricultural
- **Notes:** Program closed; one-time grant opportunity for local governments to support clean energy projects

## Wellesley Municipal Light Plant (WMLP)
**Source:** http://wellesleyma.gov/799/Energy-Efficiency-Rebates
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** 

### Electrification and Efficiency Rebates
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $100, $250, $500, up to $1,500, up to $2,000, up to $3,000
- **Eligibility:** Wellesley residents with active electric accounts in good standing; new construction projects ineligible; National Grid gas customers not eligible for WMLP rebates for certain equipment
- **How to Apply:** Online form or mail-in PDF version with supporting documentation (receipt or invoice, yellow Energy Guide label)
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial
- **Notes:** Update Effective 1/1/26: 75% up to $2,000; 75% up to $3,000 if weatherization is completed within 6 months of installing a qualifying whole-home heat pump system

### Heat Pump Program
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Eligibility:** Wellesley residents with active electric accounts in good standing; new construction projects ineligible
- **How to Apply:** Link to online form or mail-in PDF version
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial

### Bring Your Own Charger (BYOC) Program
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $250; $125 for EV chargers purchased 1/1/26 or later
- **Eligibility:** Wellesley residents with active electric accounts in good standing; National Grid gas customers eligible
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial

### MassSave Rebates (referenced on WMLP page)
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Eligibility:** National Grid gas customers eligible for rebates and not WMLP rebates; new construction projects ineligible
- **How to Apply:** Link to online form or mail-in PDF version
- **Sector:** Residential, Commercial, Industrial

## Maryland Energy Administration
**Source:** http://energy.maryland.gov/business/Pages/incentives/empowermdcigp.aspx
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** 

### Commercial & Industrial Grant Program
- **Type:** grant
- **Financial Details:** $8,800,000 program budget
- **Eligibility:** commercial and industrial facilities; existing or new construction; facilities used predominantly by low income populations receive 100% project cost reimbursement
- **How to Apply:** application deadline is January 30 for FY26 (September 2026 for FY27); MEA received 58 FY26 C&I applications, evaluation and scoring are complete; awards will be issued to top-scoring applications until program budget is depleted
- **Sector:** ['Commercial', 'Industrial']

- **Type:** Grant, Low-Interest Loan
- **Financial Details:** $2 million in funding for projects that improve energy efficiency and reduce energy costs.
- **Eligibility:** Commercial and Industrial facilities, must be located within Montana, and must meet certain energy efficiency goals.
- **How to Apply:** Application can be made online through the EPC website or by contacting a DEQ representative.
- **Sector:** ['Commercial', 'Industrial']

### Multifamily Energy Retrofit Loan Program (MERL)
- **Type:** Grant, Low-Interest Loan
- **Financial Details:** $1.5 million in funding for projects that improve energy efficiency and reduce energy costs in multifamily buildings.
- **Eligibility:** Multifamily building owners and managers, must be located within Montana, and must meet certain energy efficiency goals.
- **How to Apply:** Application can be made online through the MERL website or by contacting a DEQ representative.
- **Sector:** ['Residential']

- **Financial Details:** $500,000 in funding for projects that improve energy efficiency and increase use of renewable energy sources.
- **Eligibility:** Commercial and Industrial facilities, must be located within Montana, and must meet certain energy efficiency or renewable energy goals.
- **How to Apply:** Application can be made online through the Renewable Energy Program website or by contacting a DEQ representative.

### Electrification and Efficiency Rebate
- **Type:** rebate
- **Financial Details:** $100, $250, $500, 50% up to $1,500, 50% up to $2,500, 75% up to $2,000, 75% up to $3,000, $250
- **Eligibility:** Active electric account in good standing with the WMLP (at least 6 months of satisfactory payments), new equipment only, no temporary, mobile, or portable equipment, must be installed within a specific timeframe depending on the rebate type
- **How to Apply:** Submit rebate application within six (6) months of equipment purchase or weatherization installation, provide proof of purchase and brand/model information where applicable, WMLP may verify installation
- **Sector:** ['Residential']
- **Notes:** Customer accounts are subject to rebate limits, rebates for new construction projects are not available, rebates will be issued as a check, sales tax and installation costs (except weatherization) not included, processing time is 4-6 weeks

## Central Alabama Electric Cooperative
**Source:** http://caec.coop/member-benefits-services/heat-pumps/
**Type:** Main Link
**Summary:** The page provides details on heat pump rebates offered by Central Alabama Electric Cooperative to residential customers for both standard homes and manufactured homes. The rebates apply to dual fuel systems, mini-split units, and conversions from electric furnaces to heat pumps.

### Heat Pump Rebates - Dual Fuel Systems and Mini-Split Units for Standard Homes
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $350/ton, $350 per ton for 15 SEER or greater
- **Eligibility:** Residential customers with standard homes installing dual fuel systems or mini-split units. Installer must have a business license and perform load calculations.
- **How to Apply:** Contact CAEC at 1-800-545-5735 ext. 2118 for more information. Provide proof of purchase (invoice).
- **Sector:** Residential

### Heat Pump Rebates - Manufactured Homes
- **Type:** Rebate
- **Financial Details:** $400-700 based on tonnage, $400 per ton for converting from electric furnace to heat pump, $250/ton for 14 SEER mini-split, $350/ton for 15+ SEER mini-splits
- **Eligibility:** Residential customers with manufactured homes installing or replacing units. New installations require replacing an electric furnace with a heat pump. Conversions from electric furnaces to heat pumps are eligible.
- **How to Apply:** Contact CAEC at 1-800-545-5735 ext. 2118 for more information. Provide proof of purchase (invoice) and complete an inspection of skirting by CAEC if applicable.
- **Sector:** Residential
